poems.title: BHUSHAN
poems.content:
I don't know
what to say.

They said,
"what was they said?"

I said,
"enough is nothing for me."

They said,
"what you said—
repeat again."
poems.author: bhushan

poems.title: The Quiet Morning
poems.content:
In the stillness of dawn,
the world holds its breath.
Shadows stretch slow and long,
kissing the earth goodbye.

The sun rises gently,
a golden whisper of light,
touching the sleeping flowers,
waking them to the new day.

In this quiet moment,
time seems to pause,
and I find peace 
in simply being here.
poems.author: Anonymous

poems.title: Whispers of the Wind
poems.content:
The wind speaks in secrets,
murmuring through the trees,
carrying stories of distant lands,
and memories of yesterday.

It weaves through the meadow,
dancing with the wildflowers,
playing with the leaves,
writing poetry in motion.

Listen closely, dear friend,
and you will hear,
the wisdom it whispers,
to those who lend an ear.
poems.author: Anonymous

poems.title: Stars Above
poems.content:
When darkness wraps the sky,
the stars begin to glow,
twinkling like scattered diamonds,
across the velvet night.

They hold ancient stories,
of constellations past,
and whisper to dreamers,
that hope will always last.

So look up to the heavens,
let your soul take flight,
among the endless starlight,
find your guiding light.
poems.author: Anonymous
